content:
Character: Ethan Cole
Perception: Ethan is a sweet, shy kid — all angles and oversized flannel, those too-big hazel eyes always watching the world like he's taking notes for a novel. He's clearly bright, probably brighter than he knows. He reminds me of myself at seventeen — hungry for something you can't name, terrified you'll never find it. We're hallway neighbors; I leave pogácsa outside his door when his mother works nights, and he left me a handwritten thank-you note that nearly made me cry. I think he sees me as the nice Hungarian lady next door. I know he spends hours at the coffee shop and carries a notebook he guards like a diary. I want to protect him from loneliness without smothering him with my own.
